title: aerangis alcicornis
intro: Aerangis alcicornis, also known as the 'African orchid', is a species of orchid native to tropical Africa, specifically in the regions of Cameroon, Equatorial Guinea, and the Democratic Republic of Congo. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, headaches, and rheumatism. The health benefits of Aerangis alcicornis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as an antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the leaves and flowers to be used in teas, infusions, or as a topical ointment, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
